class.name: AsioAudioInput
class.brief_description: Low-latency audio input from an ASIO driver (Windows only).

method.name: close
method.description: Stops the stream (if running), disposes the ASIO buffers and releases the driver. Safe to call multiple times. Called automatically when the node is freed.

method.name: get_buffer_size
method.description: Returns the buffer size, in samples per channel, that the driver was opened with. Valid after a successful [method open]. Smaller values mean lower latency. See also [method get_min_buffer_size].

method.name: get_driver_list
method.description: Returns the names of all installed ASIO drivers. Pass one of these names to [method open]. Returns an empty array if no ASIO drivers are installed.

method.name: get_input_channels
method.description: Returns the number of input channels available on the opened driver. Valid after a successful [method open]. [signal audio_frame] is emitted once per channel, with [code]channel[/code] ranging from [code]0[/code] to this value minus one.

method.name: get_min_buffer_size
method.description: Returns the minimum buffer size, in samples, that the opened driver supports. Pass [code]-1[/code] to [method open] to use this size for the lowest possible input latency. Valid after a successful [method open].

method.name: get_sample_rate
method.description: Returns the sample rate, in Hz, that the driver is running at. Valid after a successful [method open]. Match an [AudioStreamGenerator]'s [code]mix_rate[/code] to this value to avoid resampling.

method.name: is_running
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] while the audio stream is running (between [method start] and [method stop]).

method.name: open
method.description: Loads and initializes the named ASIO driver (one of the names from [method get_driver_list]) and creates input buffers. Returns [code]true[/code] on success. [param buffer_size] selects the samples-per-block: [code]0[/code] uses the driver's preferred size (default), [code]-1[/code] uses the minimum size for lowest latency, and any positive value is clamped to the driver's supported range and rounded to its granularity. Call [method start] afterwards to begin capturing. Calling [method open] again first closes any previously opened driver.

method.name: show_control_panel
method.description: Opens the driver's own settings panel, if it provides one. Many drivers expose buffer size and sample rate configuration here. Requires a driver to be opened first.

method.name: start
method.description: Starts the audio stream. Must be called after a successful [method open]. Returns [code]true[/code] on success. Once started, [signal audio_frame] begins firing.

method.name: stop
method.description: Stops the audio stream. The driver remains open and can be restarted with [method start]. Does nothing if not running.

signal.name: audio_frame
signal.description: Emitted once per input channel for every captured audio block. [param data] holds the block's samples as normalized floats (roughly in the [code]-1.0[/code]..[code]1.0[/code] range), and [param channel] identifies the source channel ([code]0[/code]-based). Driver sample formats (16/24/32-bit integer, 32/64-bit float) are converted to float automatically. Emitted on the main thread, so it is safe to call into the scene tree from a connected handler.

signal.name: reset_requested
signal.description: Emitted when the driver requests a reset (for example after a configuration change in its control panel). Respond by calling [method close] and then [method open]/[method start] again.

signal.name: sample_rate_changed
signal.description: Emitted when the driver reports a sample rate change, usually due to external clock sync. [param new_rate] is the new rate in Hz. If you feed an [AudioStreamGenerator], rebuild it with the new [code]mix_rate[/code].
